A resolution introduced by Coun-
Gilman Lambert was adopted by the
city council last night providing for
the sale of liquor in restaurants in
connection with meals, on the pay
ment of $500 license fee. The re-
tail License is $1,000.


The city council has confirmed
Edward Riley as plumbing inspec-
tor under the new city plumbing
ordinance. Herman Siehart and
Harry Chisholm have been con-
firmed as his deputies.


Wilbur Wester, a Tacoma capital,
list and real estate promoter, is in
Spokane looking for large body of
land close to the city to be used in
placing on the market tracts for
small farms and country home
IL 10 odlu IlldI hC 10 ~
pared to invest from 100000 to
100000 Wester recently sold a
large body of land for nearly mil
lion dollars On the electric line be-
tween Seattle and Tacoma, which
is now being built up with fine
country residences.


Spokane council No. 693, Knights
of Columbus, has chartered a spe-
cial train on the Inland and will go
to Occur dAlene next Sunday after
noon to attend the second initia-
ton of Kootenai council of that
city. The special will leave Spo-
kane at p. m. and John Skerritt,
financial secretary of the Spokane
council, has the train arrangements
in hand. a class of 60 candidates
will be given the first, second and
third degrees.


Judge J. D. HinkIe of the super
ior court determined yesterday that
the city must pay % cents a square
foot for the 5000 square feet re-
CURED to widen Sheridan street and
the 30.560 square feet required to
widen First avenue, total of $780,
690S0.
